---
title: "WALKER_Assignment_9"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-30"
---

Luke Walker's model comparision of Graduate Data.

First we will load up our data and tools. We are reading a set of 4 varibles, which include wither or not they were given admission into the graduate program with admit, their GRE, their GPA, and finally their rank with "1" being the best. 

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

grad <- read.csv("Data/GradSchool_Admissions.csv")

print(grad)
str(grad)
View(grad)

```

After looking at the data, we wanted to see if we could draw conclusions that GRE, GPA, and Rank had anything to do with a 1 value in admin.

```{r}
mod1 <- glm(admit ~ gre + gpa + rank, data = grad, family = binomial)
summary(mod1)

mod2 <- glm(admit ~ gre + gpa, data = grad, family = binomial)
summary(mod2)

mod3 <- glm(admit ~ gpa + rank, data = grad, family = binomial)
summary(mod3)

mod4 <- glm(admit ~ gre + rank, data = grad, family = binomial)
summary(mod4)

```

 Summaries of all the models. 

```{r}

AIC(mod1, mod2, mod3, mod4)

```

Looking at the values here with the AIC function, you can see that model 1 has the lowest value, making it the sharpest model to use. That makes sense because all the variales were used in model 1 compared to the other models.






Stuff at the bottom. 


IGNORE PAST HERE. 

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
